english: of the provisions Dzl.; dbui ča tsam čig gsér-gyis ma lóṅ-bar (dbu'i cha tsam cig gser gyis ma long bar) there being still wanting about as much gold as (the weight of) his head Glr.; náṅ-par sṅá-bai ča (nang par snga ba'i cha) the following day's first part, i.e. the following morning Mil.; sá-ča (sa cha) a piece of land Glr., C., also land, territory, country in general, ghai sa-ča (ghai sa cha) the country of Gha Glr.; zúr-ča (zur cha) frontier parts, frontier province; ča-snyoms (cha snyoms) at equal parts, equally, e.g. ču sbyar (chu sbyar) mixed with the same quantity of water Lt.; ča-mnyám (cha mnyam) id., ča-mnyám žib bteg (cha mnyam zhib bteg) accurately weighed in equal parts Lt.; ča tsam (cha tsam), ča °dra tsam (cha 'dra tsam) in part, in some measure; ča ma °dra (cha ma 'dra) or ma mtún-pa (ma mthun pa) partly not equal, differing a little; ča tsam śes kyaṅ (cha tsam shes kyang) even if one knows but a little Mil.; yid smon °os ča tsam mi gdá-bas (yid smon 'os cha tsam mi gda' bas) it being not in the least desirable; ča-rdzógs (cha rdzogs) being complete in every part, entire, integral Sch. — Esp. 2. the half, nám-gyi ča stod (nam gyi cha stod), the first half of the night, nám-gyi ča smad (nam gyi cha smad) the second, the last half of it. Hence 3. the one part of a pair, similar to ya (ya), lham ča gčig (lham cha gcig) the one boot; ča sgrig-pa (cha sgrig pa) to pair, to match, to couple Sch.; an equal, a match, ča-mtún-pa (cha mthun pa), ča-°drá-ba (cha 'dra ba), C. also *ča-lóṅ-wa* (*cha long wa*), similar, resembling Wdn. and elsewh.; la-lá t́ar-pa ča-mtún dge-ba med (la la thar pa cha mthun dge ba med) some have no virtue befitting (i.e. leading to) final salvation Thgy.; ča-méd (cha med) without an equal, matchless; čá-ma-yin-pa (cha ma yin pa) unfit, improper, unbecoming Sch., ṅag gčóg-pa ni ča ma yin (ngag gcog pa ni cha ma yin) not obeying will not do, is out of place Tar. 110, 11. — 4. a pair, = zuṅ (zung) Sch.; Zam.: युग. — 5. share, portion, lot, mtsar-sdúg bltá-bai čá-nas mnyam (mtshar sdug blta ba'i cha nas mnyam) being equal as to their (respective) share of beauty Glr.; dmán-ča °dzín-pa (dman cha 'dzin pa) to choose the humbler (inferior) share, i.e. to be humble,= dmán-sa °dzín-pa (dman sa 'dzin pa) Mil.; in general: ča °dzín-pa (cha 'dzin pa) c. genit. to adhere, to be attached to a person or thing Pth.; žiṅ rmó-ba ṅai ča yin (zhing rmo ba nga'i cha yin) ploughing is my business, my lot, my department Dzl.; čá-la (cha la) equally, in equal parts, equally divided, k̇á-ba nyin dgu mtsan dgu babs (kha ba nyin dgu mtshan dgu babs), čá-la nyin mtsan bco-brgyád babs (cha la nyin mtshan bco brgyad babs) Mil. there was a fall of snow during nine days and nine nights; it fell equally portioned out to days and nights, (together) eighteen (the peculiar mode of reckoning is here to be noticed) II. news, intelligence, notice, construed like rgyus (rgyus) and gtam (gtam); gtám-ča °dri-ba (gtam cha 'dri ba) = gtam °dri-ba (gtam 'dri ba); ča yod (cha yod), ča med (cha med) like rgyus yod (rgyus yod) and rgyus med (rgyus med); nam °či ča méd-kyi čos (nam 'chi cha med kyi chos) the doctrine of the uncertainty of the day of death Mil.; ... par ča mčis-te (... par cha mchis te) there coming news or intelligence that ...; skád-ča (skad cha) v. skad (skad); physically: voice, sound, brág-ča (brag cha) echo; intellectually: prospect, auspices, Mil.: sróg-ča (srog cha) prospects of life (as to its length and preservation), kyím-ča (khyim cha) prospects regarding the household, dgra-ča (dgra cha) prospects, expectations as to one's enemies; *lám-ča* (*lam cha*) C. prospects of a safe journey (cf. no 4). III. thing, things, relating to clothes, ornaments, materials etc., cf. čas (chas); *go-lus-ča-tsáṅ* (*go lus cha tshang*) W. a complete suit of clothes; but mostly used in compounds: ské-ča (ske cha) neck-ornaments, glo-ča (glo cha) ornaments suspended to the belt or girdle, e.g. strings of shells; dgós-ča (dgos cha) necessary things Cs.; mčód-ča (mchod cha) things necessary for sacrifices, requisites for offerings Glr.; mtsón-ča (mtshon cha) weapons; yig-ča (yig cha) prob. writings, deeds, documents Glr.; *rẹ̄-ča* (*re cha*) cottons, cotton fabrics C.; lag-ča (lag cha) implements, utensils, goods, baggage etc. Glr. — There is still to be noticed the expression: ča-bžág-pa (cha bzhag pa), lit. to add one's own share to a thing, 1. to adhere, stick, or cling to, to follow, obey (laws); saṅs-rgyás-kyi bk̇á-la ča bžag (sangs rgyas kyi bka' la cha bzhag) they adhere to the words of Buddha; rgyál-poi bk̇á-la (rgyal po'i bka' la) to obey the king's commandment. 2. to refer to (?) C.
Lex., Sch.: 'share of destiny, of fate; consequence of one's actions' (?).
Mil., hem, edge, border; čá-ga °debs-pa (cha ga 'debs pa) to hem, to turn in (the edge of cloth).
